hero.tagline: A compact SUV-style hatch with a high stance, broad powertrain choice and strong safety story.

pricing.notes: Variant prices are ex-showroom reference prices from public Indian variant-price listings; verify against Tata Motors city/dealer pricing before publishing final on-road values.

overview.minides: Small in size, big on confidence — the Punch delivers SUV styling, practicality, and 5-star safety.
overview.description: The Tata Punch is a compact micro-SUV that blends SUV styling, city-friendly dimensions, and strong safety credentials. Available with petrol and CNG powertrains, manual and AMT gearboxes, it stands out with its bold design, 5-star safety rating, high ground clearance, and practical everyday usability.

dimensions.bootSpace: 366 Litres petrol / reduced in CNG variants with twin-cylinder layout
dimensions.fuelTank: 37 L petrol / 60 L water equivalent CNG

suspension.front: Independent, lower wishbone, McPherson strut with coil spring
suspension.rear: Semi-independent twist beam with coil spring and shock absorber

blog.title: Tata Punch review: compact size, big-car stance and many variants
blog.subtitle: A practical city SUV with petrol, CNG, AMT and turbo options.
blog.summary: The Punch is aimed at buyers who want a compact footprint without giving up ground clearance, cabin practicality or safety. Its biggest strength is choice: the range covers simple entry variants, sunroof-equipped mid variants, CNG automatic choices and a stronger turbo-petrol manual.

sections.heading: Design and road presence
body: The Punch carries Tata's SUV-inspired stance in a sub-four-metre body. The upright bonnet, cladded wheel arches and high seating position make it feel more substantial than a regular hatchback, while the compact length keeps it easy to place in traffic.

sections.heading: Engines and variants
body: The standard 1.2L petrol is available with manual and AMT options, while the CNG range adds both manual and AMT choices on selected personas. Buyers wanting stronger performance can pick the turbo-petrol manual in Adventure and Accomplished+ S forms.
The official brochure matrix lists 26 combinations across Smart, Pure, Pure+, Pure+ S, Adventure, Adventure S, Accomplished and Accomplished+ S personas.

sections.heading: Features and comfort
body: Lower variants cover the basics such as AC, power steering, front power windows, central locking and core safety equipment. Mid and higher variants add touchscreen infotainment, wireless phone projection, rear camera, cruise control, rear AC vents, alloy wheels, connected tech, wireless charging and a voice-assisted sunroof depending on trim.

sections.heading: Safety and ownership
body: The Punch has built much of its reputation on crash-test performance and standard safety equipment. ESP, dual airbags, ABS with EBD, ISOFIX, rear parking sensors and TPMS are represented across the range, with six airbags reserved for the top Accomplished+ S family in this data file.

pros: Wide 26-variant spread covers petrol, CNG, AMT and turbo needs
High seating position and compact dimensions suit Indian city use
Strong safety reputation and useful standard safety equipment
CNG automatic availability improves daily-use convenience
cons: Naturally aspirated petrol can feel relaxed with a full load
Top features are concentrated in higher personas
No diesel, EV or AWD option in the ICE Punch range
blog.verdict: The Tata Punch is best for buyers who want a compact, easy-to-drive car with SUV-like confidence and a broad choice of fuel and gearbox options. Pure+ and Adventure variants are sensible value picks, while Accomplished+ S is the fully loaded choice.

faqs.question: How many variants are listed for the Tata Punch in this JSON?
faqs.answer: This file contains 26 variants, matching the official brochure's drivetrain-persona matrix.

faqs.question: Does Tata Punch have CNG automatic variants?
faqs.answer: Yes. The file includes CNG AMT variants in Pure+, Adventure, Adventure S and Accomplished+ S families.

faqs.question: Does Tata Punch have ADAS?
faqs.answer: No. ADAS features are marked false in the variant feature sections.

metadata.notes: Tata's brochure shows 26 variants across 8 persona labels. The optional trims array groups those into 6 buyer-facing groups for websites that need six trim tabs.
